_doc: Curated list of packages with publicly documented supply-chain compromise events.
Even after the malicious version is cleaned up, this list keeps the institutional memory.
Each entry: package + ecosystem + incident date + brief description + reference URL.
Sources: GHSA, Snyk advisories, Sonatype reports, npm security blog, PyPI advisories.

incidents.name: event-stream
incidents.description: New maintainer added the malicious dependency 'flatmap-stream' which targeted Bitcoin wallets in Copay app

incidents.name: flatmap-stream
incidents.description: Created specifically to be added as a malicious dependency to event-stream; stole Bitcoin wallets

incidents.name: ua-parser-js
incidents.description: Maintainer's npm account was hijacked; cryptominer + password stealer added

incidents.name: coa
incidents.description: Maintainer's account hijacked; identical malware to ua-parser-js incident

incidents.name: rc
incidents.description: Maintainer's npm account hijacked, similar payload to ua-parser-js

incidents.name: colors
incidents.description: Author intentionally introduced an infinite loop ('protestware'); broke many builds

incidents.name: faker
incidents.description: Author wiped the package and added intentional 'protestware' content

incidents.name: node-ipc
incidents.description: Author introduced code that wiped files on systems geolocated to Russia/Belarus

incidents.name: @ctrl/tinycolor
incidents.description: Phishing of the maintainer's npm account; malicious crypto-stealer payload added (Shai-Hulud worm)

incidents.name: ctx
incidents.description: Original maintainer's domain expired; attacker re-registered, took over PyPI account, added credential exfil

incidents.name: phpass
incidents.description: Same campaign as ctx — domain takeover led to malicious upload with credential exfil

incidents.name: request
incidents.version_affected: all (this is a typosquat package; 'requests' is the legit one)
incidents.description: Typosquat of 'requests' - any version is suspicious; never publish a project with this dep name

incidents.name: colourama
incidents.description: Typosquat of 'colorama' - canonical PyPI typosquat case study

incidents.name: xz-utils
incidents.description: Two-year campaign by 'Jia Tan' to gain maintainer access and plant a backdoor enabling SSH RCE on systemd-using Linux systems
